lines.content: # Commenting I/O redirection tests as JUnit testing is used.
# - name: Build and check with Gradle
#   run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y dos2unix
# - name: Perform IO redirection test (*NIX or macOS)

A CLI-based Stock Keeping Unit (sku) Ticketing System. A localized command-line tool designed to handle inventory specific actions required for individual item SKUs such as damage checks, expiry reviews & quality control. Unlike standard commercial inventory software that tracks quantity, ItemTracker tracks accountability & actions, allowing managers to attach specific tasks with priorities to individual SKUs.
* Represents the specific sectors within the warehouse grid.
* Represents a Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) in the inventory ticketing system.
* Contains the SKU identifier, its physical location in the warehouse, and a list of tasks assigned to it.
* Initializes a new SKU with the specified ID and location.
* An empty task list is automatically created for the SKU upon instantiation.
* @param skuID The unique alphanumeric identifier for the SKU.
* @param skuLocation The physical sector location of the SKU in the warehouse.
* Retrieves the identifier of the SKU.
* Retrieves the physical warehouse location of the SKU.
* @return The Location enum representing the SKU's placement.
* Retrieves the list of tasks assigned to this SKU.
* @return The SKUTaskList containing all tasks associated with the SKU.
* Represents a list of Stock Keeping Units (SKUs).
* Provides operations to manage the collection of SKUs, such as adding and deleting.
* Initializes an empty list to store SKUs.
* Retrieves the total number of SKUs currently in the list.
* @return The integer count of SKUs being tracked.
* Checks whether the SKU list is completely empty.
* @return True if there are no SKUs in the list, false otherwise.
* Adds a new SKU with the specified ID and location to the list.
* @param skuID The unique alphanumeric identifier for the new SKU.
* @param skuLocation The physical sector location of the new SKU.

* Executes {@link ParsedCommand} instances against the warehouse data model.

*

Design note: Since {@code SKU} does not yet hold a {@code SKUTaskList}
* field directly (it is commented out in SKU.java), this class maintains an internal
* {@code HashMap<String, SKUTaskList>} keyed by SKU_ID. Once Om's team uncomments
* and wires in {@code SKUTaskList} inside {@code SKU}, this map can be replaced by
* delegating straight to the {@code SKU} object.
* which requires adding a {@code getSKUList()} method to {@code SKUList.java}:
/** Set to false to stop the main loop. */

* Remove this once SKU holds its own SKUTaskList.
* Constructs a CommandRunner backed by the given {@link SKUList}.
* @param skuList The shared SKU data store.
* Returns {@code true} while the application should keep running.
* Dispatches a {@link ParsedCommand} to the appropriate handler.
* @param cmd The parsed command to execute.
assert cmd != null : "ParsedCommand should not be null";
* Validates that the location is a legal 3x3 sector and that the SKU is not a duplicate.
Ui.printError("Usage: addsku n/SKU_ID l/LOCATION  (e.g. addsku n/WIDGET-A1 l/B2)");
Ui.printError("Invalid location '" + locationStr + "'. Must be one of: A1 A2 A3 B1 B2 B3 C1 C2 C3");
Ui.printSuccess("Added SKU [" + skuId.toUpperCase() + "] at location " + location);
* Also removes all tasks associated with the SKU.
Ui.printSuccess("Deleted SKU [" + skuId.toUpperCase() + "] and all its tasks.");
* Priority defaults to HIGH if {@code p/} flag is omitted.
Ui.printError("Invalid priority '" + cmd.getArg("p") + "'. Use HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.");
Ui.printSuccess("Added task #" + newIndex + " to SKU [" + skuId.toUpperCase()
* Removes the task at the 1-based index shown by {@code listtasks}.
Ui.printError("Task index " + index + " is out of range for SKU: " + skuId);
Ui.printSuccess("Deleted task #" + index + " from SKU [" + skuId.toUpperCase() + "].");
* Marks the task at the given 1-based index as completed.
Ui.printError("Task index " + index + " is out of range for SKU: " + skuId);
Ui.printSuccess("Marked task #" + index + " as done for SKU [" + skuId.toUpperCase() + "].");
* Unmarks the task at the given 1-based index.
Ui.printError("Task index " + index + " is out of range for SKU: " + skuId);
Ui.printSuccess("Unmarked task #" + index + " for SKU [" + skuId.toUpperCase() + "].");

// Build a list of [skuId, task, distanceToSku] tuples

* Lists every task in the system, grouped by SKU.

Note for Om's team: This method requires {@code SKUList.getSKUList()} to return

Ui.printError("Task index must be a number, got: '" + indexStr + "'");

* Main entry-point for the java.duke.ItemTasker application.
* Represents a fully parsed user command, containing:

*
  • A map of flag keys to values (e.g. {@code "n" -> "COMPUTER-MOUSE-X1"})
* @param commandWord The command keyword in lowercase.
* @param args        Map of flag keys (without slash) to their string values.
public ParsedCommand(String commandWord, Map<String, String> args) {
* Returns the value for the given flag key, or {@code null} if not present.
* Returns whether the given flag key is present in this command.
assert input != null : "Input Should not be null";
private static Map<String, String> parseFlags(String argsString) {
// Split at boundaries like " n/" " p/" " d/" " l/" " i/"
* Handles all console input and output for ItemTasker.
* Centralises printing so that formatting changes only need to be made here.
* Reads and returns the next line of user input, trimmed.
/** Prints the welcome banner on application startup. */
System.out.println(" ItemTasker — CLI SKU Ticketing System");
System.out.println(" Type a command to get started. Type 'help' for a list of commands.");
/** Prints the goodbye message on application exit. */
System.out.println(" Goodbye! All tasks have been saved.");
* Prints a success message prefixed with [OK].
* Prints an error message prefixed with [ERROR].
* Prints an informational message prefixed with [INFO].
* Prints a message when the command entered is not recognised.
System.out.println(" [ERROR] Unknown command: '" + command + "'. Type 'help' for valid commands.");
/** Prints the help reference listing all valid commands and their formats. */
System.out.println("   addsku n/SKU_ID l/LOCATION          Add a SKU to the warehouse.");
System.out.println("   deletesku n/SKU_ID                  Remove a SKU and all its tasks.");
System.out.println("   addskutask n/SKU_ID d/DUE_DATE      Add a HIGH priority task (default).");
System.out.println("         [p/PRIORITY]                  Optional: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.");
System.out.println("   deletetask n/SKU_ID i/TASK_INDEX    Delete task at given index.");
System.out.println("   marktask n/SKU_ID i/TASK_INDEX      Mark a task as completed.");
System.out.println("   unmarktask n/SKU_ID i/TASK_INDEX    Unmark a completed task.");
System.out.println("   listtasks n/SKU_ID                  List tasks for a specific SKU.");
System.out.println("   listtasks p/PRIORITY                List tasks filtered by priority.");
System.out.println("   listtasks l/LOCATION                List tasks sorted by distance.");
System.out.println("   help                                Show this help message.");
System.out.println(" Locations: A1 A2 A3 | B1 B2 B3 | C1 C2 C3  (3x3 warehouse grid)");
// Initialize a fresh list before each test
// Verify the remaining SKU is the correct one
// Attempting to delete an ID that doesn't exist
// The list size should remain exactly the same
assertNotNull(sku.getSKUTaskList(), "Task list should be initialized upon SKU creation.");
assertTrue(sku.getSKUTaskList().isEmpty(), "Newly created SKU should have an empty task list.");
